name: Zhores I. Alferov
reason: for developing semiconductor heterostructures used in high-speed- and opto-electronics

name: Yves Chauvin
reason: for the development of the metathesis method in organic synthesis

name: Yuan T. Lee
reason: for their contributions concerning the dynamics of chemical elementary processes

name: Yoshinori Ohsumi
reason: for his discoveries of mechanisms for autophagy

name: Yoichiro Nambu
reason: for the discovery of the mechanism of spontaneous broken symmetry in subatomic physics

name: Yitzhak Rabin
reason: for their efforts to create peace in the Middle East

name: Yasunari Kawabata
reason: for his narrative mastery, which with great sensibility expresses the essence of the Japanese mind

name: Yasser Arafat
reason: for their efforts to create peace in the Middle East

name: World Food Programme
reason: for its efforts to combat hunger, for its contribution to bettering conditions for peace in conflict-affected areas and for acting as a driving force in efforts to prevent the use of hunger as a weapon of war and conflict

name: Thomas Woodrow Wilson
reason: for his role as founder of the League of Nations

name: Wolfgang Pauli
reason: for the discovery of the Exclusion Principle, also called the Pauli Principle

name: Wolfgang Paul
reason: for the development of the ion trap technique

name: Wolfgang Ketterle
reason: for the achievement of Bose-Einstein condensation in dilute gases of alkali atoms, and for early fundamental studies of the properties of the condensates

name: Wole Soyinka
reason: who in a wide cultural perspective and with poetic overtones fashions the drama of existence

name: Władysław Stanisław Reymont
reason: for his great national epic, The Peasants
